text: Annual wellness/health maintenance visit performed
transcript_quote: So today is your yearly check — the full once-over.

text: Patient reports feeling mostly well since last year
transcript_quote: Thanks be to God, mostly good. I keep busy.

text: Knee osteoarthritis currently asymptomatic; worse in winter, better in summer
transcript_quote: Today, quiet. Zero complaints... it's the winter when it creaks like my kitchen door.

text: Naproxen 220 mg oral tablet, one with breakfast as needed for knee pain, taken with food; some weeks none
transcript_quote: I take the naproxen, the little blue ones from the pharmacy, 220, one with breakfast, and that settles it.

text: Denies GI red flags from NSAID use: no stomach burning, no black stools; no knee swelling
transcript_quote: No stomach burning, no black stools, no swelling in the knee?

text: Denies chest pain, shortness of breath, and palpitations
transcript_quote: Any chest pain, shortness of breath, palpitations?

text: Walks to market 15 minutes each way, uphill on return, without limitation
transcript_quote: I walk to the market and back, fifteen minutes each way, no problem. Uphill on the way home

text: Denies dizziness, headaches, vision changes, and numbness
transcript_quote: Any dizziness, headaches, changes in your vision, numbness?

text: Sleep: sleeps well until 5 a.m. then early morning worrying/waking for ~20 minutes; appetite normal
transcript_quote: I sleep like a stone until five, then the worrying shift begins.

text: PHQ-2 depression screen negative, score 0
transcript_quote: In the past two weeks, little interest or pleasure in things you usually enjoy?

text: GAD-7 anxiety screen score 5, mild anxiety
transcript_quote: That puts your anxiety score at a five — mild territory.

text: HARK domestic abuse screen negative, score 0; patient reports feeling safe at home
transcript_quote: In my house now it is me, my daughter, and three noisy angels. I am safe. I promise you that.

text: Prior history of intimate partner violence with first husband, no longer in her life
transcript_quote: That was my first husband, many years ago, another lifetime. He is long gone from my life.

text: Tobacco status: ex-smoker, quit ~50 years ago after brief teenage use
transcript_quote: Two years of smoking when I was a teenager... That was almost fifty years ago.

text: Alcohol use minimal (a cup of wine at Christmas); DAST-10 drug screen score 0
transcript_quote: A little cup of wine at Christmas. Otherwise no, nothing, never.

text: Medication reconciliation completed; only naproxen 220 mg PRN plus manzanilla (chamomile) tea, no other supplements
transcript_quote: Reconciled and official.

text: Blood pressure 135/82 mmHg, mildly elevated systolic, not requiring medication
transcript_quote: Blood pressure today: 135 over 82. The top number is a touch above perfect

text: Heart rate 60/min, respiratory rate 12/min
transcript_quote: Heart rate 60 and steady, breathing 12

text: Weight 73.8 kg, down from prior 78 kg; BMI 27.7
transcript_quote: your weight is 73.8 kilos... your BMI is 27.7 now.

text: Hemoglobin A1c 6.11%, consistent with prediabetes, stable from last year
transcript_quote: the A1c — is 6.11 percent. Still prediabetes, same neighborhood as last year

text: Random blood glucose 79 mg/dL
transcript_quote: Today's sugar was 79, which is lovely.

text: Creatinine 0.68 mg/dL, normal renal function
transcript_quote: Kidneys perfect — creatinine 0.68.

text: Sodium 138 mmol/L, potassium 3.9 mmol/L
transcript_quote: Sodium 138, potassium 3.9.

text: Lipid panel: total cholesterol 168, LDL 87, HDL 59, triglycerides 110 mg/dL; well-controlled hyperlipidemia
transcript_quote: Total cholesterol 168, the bad LDL is 87, the good HDL is 59, triglycerides 110.

text: CBC: hemoglobin 16.9 g/dL, hematocrit 47.6%, platelets 351, WBC 7.4; no anemia, prior anemia resolved
transcript_quote: hemoglobin 16.9, hematocrit 47.6 — no sign of the old anemia today

text: Oral exam shows puffy lower gums that bleed on touch and with brushing; diagnosed gingivitis
transcript_quote: Your gums are puffy along the bottom, and they bleed a little when I touch them... it's gingivitis, the early, fixable kind of gum disease.

text: Referral to dentist for professional cleaning for gingivitis
transcript_quote: I'm sending you to the dentist for a proper cleaning.

text: Counseled on oral hygiene: brush twice daily with soft brush, continue despite bleeding, improves in weeks
transcript_quote: Brush twice a day, soft brush, and don't stop when it bleeds — that part gets better in a couple of weeks.

text: Influenza vaccine (trivalent, split virus, preservative-free) administered in left arm
transcript_quote: your flu shot... Same one as every year, the trivalent.

text: Counseled on infection prevention: handwashing and sleep given exposure to grandchildren
transcript_quote: Wash the hands, get the sleep, and you're as covered as I can make you.

text: SDOH: lives with 5 people including daughter and 3 grandchildren; caregiver role in mornings; social isolation noted (limited social contact, 'world got small')
transcript_quote: My world got small, Doctor. Busy, but small.

text: SDOH: refugee, primary language other than English, high school education, full-time employment, private insurance, stable housing
Pain severity score 0/10
transcript_quote: Today, quiet. Zero complaints

text: Follow-up: recheck sugar/annual labs next year; continue walks and measured rice for prediabetes, oatmeal for cholesterol
transcript_quote: sugar — steady, keep the walks and the measured rice, we recheck next year.

text: Counseled to report if anxiety worsens (persistent, sleep-disrupting, or daytime)
transcript_quote: If it ever starts staying — keeping you from sleep past those twenty minutes, or following you through the day — you tell me

text: Stress finding documented (PRAPARE stress level 'somewhat'), status resolved
transcript_quote: Lying there doing arithmetic — the bills, my daughter, my sister back home.
